description: This optical micrograph presents a cross-sectional view of the oxide scale formed on Fe-20Mn-6Al alloy after isothermal oxidation at 800°C for 24 hours in pure oxygen at 200 torr pressure. The image distinctly shows bulky manganese-rich oxides (dark upper region) formed above a ferritic surface layer, which appears as a relatively featureless band beneath the oxide. The underlying substrate exhibits a ferritic microstructure with visible grain boundaries (etched), indicating the transformation of the original duplex (α+γ) structure to a ferrite surface layer depleted of austenite during oxidation. The scale structure is stratified and complex, consistent with Region II oxidation behavior. This micrograph demonstrates that duplex alloys containing austenite suffer from poor oxidation resistance, as the austenite promotes breakdown of protective alumina scales and growth of bulky manganese-rich oxides. The Fe-20Mn-6Al alloy, despite forming this ferrite surface layer, exhibited rapid oxidation kinetics with weight gains exceeding 1 mg after 20 hours, confirming that austenite is detrimental to high-temperature oxidation resistance in Fe-Mn-Al systems.
